etymology_and_history.root_language: Language**: From "glum" + "-ness" from Middle English "gloumen" (to frown)
etymology_and_history.original_meaning: Meaning**: Frowning quality; having dejected moodiness and glumness dejection supported by cheer awareness
etymology_and_history.semantic_evolution: Evolution**: Frowning quality → dejected moodiness → contemporary glumness dejection and glumness moodiness that benefits from uplift growth
etymology_and_history.cultural_shifts: Shifts**: Frowning quality → dejected moodiness → glumness dejection → uplift understanding

contemporary_usage.primary_definition: Definition**: Having glumness dejection and glumness moodiness; experiencing dejected moodiness and glumness glumness calling for cheer uplift
contemporary_usage.register: Negative concern; indicates dejection calling for cheer support and uplift development
